tools.name: browser_open
tools.description: Open a URL in a tiny headless 'browser' session: fetch, parse the HTML via goquery, persist a cookie jar, follow redirects, and report links/forms/text. NOT a real browser — no JavaScript, no rendering, no canvas/WebGL fingerprinting. Spoofs UA + Accept-* headers from a profile (chrome/firefox/safari) but JA3/JA4 still come from the host's HTTP client. Pre-create the cache dir: `mkdir -p .cache/stado-browser`.

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Follow a link on the current page. Pick by link_index (from browser_open's links list), CSS selector, or substring text_match.

tools.name: browser_submit
tools.description: Fill and submit a form on the current page. Defaults pre-populated from the form's existing input values; overrides from `fields` win. POST forms send application/x-www-form-urlencoded; GET forms append to the URL. multipart/form-data is NOT supported in v0.1.
tools.schema: {"type":"object","properties":{"session_id":{"type":"string"},"form_index":{"type":"integer"},"selector":{"type":"string"},"fields":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":{"type":"string"}},"click":{"type":"string","description":"Name of the submit button to 'click', if the form has multiple."}},"required":["session_id"]}

tools.name: browser_screenshot
tools.description: Return the current page as text + serialized HTML + a one-line DOM summary. Not a pixel image — there's no rendering pipeline inside the wasm sandbox. Useful for an LLM walking the page.

tools.name: browser_eval
tools.description: Run a CSS selector against the current page and return matches. attr=null returns text + outerHTML; attr="href" returns the href attribute value, etc. Does NOT execute JavaScript — this is a DOM query, not eval().
